title: The EoE Do only Shinji and Asuka reject instrumentality
display_title: The EoE Do only Shinji and Asuka reject instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: The EoE - Do only Shinji and Asuka reject instrumentality?             Quote Post by Leighton » Wed Feb 16, 2011 5:36 am Was just pondering if its like another adam and eve thing after instrumentality. And oh, what happened all the other souls? Are they still as one entity in the LCL? It just seems odd that there could be billions of humans/souls but there are only 2 rejectors of instrumentality

[]: Re: The EoE - Do only Shinji and Asuka reject instrumentalit             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Feb 16, 2011 9:07 am Leighton wrote: Was just pondering if its like another adam and eve thing after instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Feb 16, 2011 9:16 am Keep in mind that we're only seeing a few minutes of time, at just one spot in the whole world, there at the end. Over time, and/or in other places, people could be coming back in droves. It's an 'open ended' ending; maybe some people come back, maybe everyone comes back (eventually), or maybe no one else at all comes back. The movie is over, so whatever happens next depends on the perspective of the audience; i.e., there is no "correct" interpretation...

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 16, 2011 10:42 am It's irrelevant whether they reject Instrumentality, because there is no more Instrumentality for them to reject. The A.T.-Fields are back and they are all individuals once more with no chance of becoming one. The choice is between being an embodied or a disolved individual. They are not in orbit. The souls are shown to fall to Earth when the black moon shatters. They are just aimlessly floating in the LCL until they decide to reembody.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Wed Feb 16, 2011 3:36 pm My personal interpretation is that Shinji was the first person to come back since he was in control and rejected instrumentality. Asuka came back because she was the first person that Shinji wanted to see. She also resolved a lot of her personal problems right before her death anyway. This is a really open ended question though, so there are a lot of ways to interpret the ending.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Feb 16, 2011 4:08 pm thewayneiac wrote: They are not in orbit. The souls are shown to fall to Earth when the black moon shatters. They are just aimlessly floating in the LCL until they decide to reembody.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 16, 2011 4:12 pm child of Lilith wrote: Going to have to disagree with this. As best as I can remember when Shinji is on the beach there is still a red ring of souls orbiting the planet. People will come out of the sea when they get their bodys back, but their not just hanging around in it.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Feb 16, 2011 4:20 pm An interesting idea to be sure, but they're probably souls. I think any blood would be long gone by now.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 16, 2011 4:40 pm Why? Lilith's blood isn't human blood, it's silly PWM nonsense. And even if it wasn't, where would it go? Blood doesn't evaporate, and shit caught in orbit doesn't eventually drift off.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Feb 16, 2011 5:02 pm And even if it wasn't, where would it go? Blood doesn't evaporate, and shit caught in orbit doesn't eventually drift off.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 16, 2011 5:40 pm This is during instrumentality: The blood shoots all the way to the moon and stains it: And then it keeps going around the Earth: Then, there's this dialogue between Rei and Shinji (both naked): Episode 26' wrote: Shinji: Ayanami... where are we? Rei: This is the sea of LCL... The primordial soup of life. A world without AT Fields... without your own shape. An ambiguous world where it is impossible to tell where you end and other people start. A fragile world where you exist everywhere, and thus exist nowhere. Shinji: Have I died? Rei: No, everything has just been joined into one. This is the world you have been hoping for... your world. Shinji (releasing Misato's cross from his left hand): But... this isn't right. I don't think this is right. Rei: If you wish once more for the existence of others, the barriers of the heart will separate everyone once more... And the fear of other people will begin again. Shinji: That's all right... (disengages Rei from him and clasps her hand) Thank you. (Shinji and Rei, Shinji's head lying in Rei's lap) Shinji: I feel that there were only hateful things there. So I'm sure it was okay to run away. But there was nothing good in the place I ran to, either. After all, I didn't exist there... which is the same as no one existing. Kaworu: Is it okay for AT Fields to hurt you and others once more? Shinji: I don't mind. But, what are you two within my heart? Rei: Hope. The hope that people might be able to understand one another. Kaworu: And the words 'I love you'. Shinji: But that's just pretending - a self-intoxicating belief... like a prayer. It can't possibly last forever. Sooner or later I'll be betrayed... And they'll leave me. Still... I want to meet them again, because I believe my feelings at that time were real.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 16, 2011 5:43 pm child of Lilith wrote: But it would fall to earth eventually. But all that's beside the point. It makes far more sense for the red ring to be souls rather then blood.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 16, 2011 9:37 pm Heh, I'm surprised anyone remembers "Fork in the Path"... I think the idea was that the souls going into Lilith's palms were channeled into Eva-01 and she runs off with them at the end (some kind of Lilith-esque time paradox), and the rest go into the Moon and are released back to Earth after HIP. It didn't have anything to do with GNR's blood-spray. I got a bit more conservative after that and decided that the best working explanation was that both the Doors of Guf and the top of the Black Moon all lead to the same place (the metaphysical Sea of LCL), though this still raises the question of why portals on two separate objects were necessary to send them there (even Leliel had one "in" and one "out"), and why all of the souls (except for a couple out of Lilith's neck when it falls off) emerge from the Black Moon. Mr. Tines wrote: On the other hand, all the goopy red dripping we saw elsewhere (from the shattered Black Moon) was clearly souls in massive quantities that hadn't yet gotten a hand-job.

[]:             Quote Post by Leighton » Thu Feb 17, 2011 9:31 am Ahh ok, that clarifies Shinji and Asuka appearing as some of the first souls on earth materialized again. So does that mean many of the other souls are stiil going through instrumentality, deciding if they are to remain as one with other souls in the LCL or if they wish to live again with AT fields? I hope in the rebuild movies we are provided with a further view after the "how disgusting" scene!

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Feb 17, 2011 10:05 am My interpretation is that Shinji sent everyone else but Asuka to hell, because he felt guilty of not understanding her feelings or something like that. I really need to re-watch EoE again.

[]:             Quote Post by slkdragon » Thu Feb 17, 2011 11:31 am Ornette wrote: This is the "Fork in the Path". So at the end of it all, when the souls that ended up in the Black Moon blow up, they all end up in the seas all over Earth, but what about the ones in Lilith? Are those in the blood that's ends up in space? Are the doors multidimensional portal-like and really everything just ends up in the Black Moon?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Feb 17, 2011 12:25 pm Leighton wrote: So does that mean many of the other souls are stiil going through instrumentality, deciding if they are to remain as one with other souls in the LCL or if they wish to live again with AT fields?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 17, 2011 1:52 pm slkdragon wrote: There's a good chance I'm recalling this incorrectly, but do we not see a few soul-like red dots drifting out of Lilith as her body breaks apart?

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Thu Feb 17, 2011 2:41 pm Maybe Yui was a lying lair who lies Shinji and Asuka are the only humans on the planet, and maybe the whole of humanity will be back soon after EoE ends. Those and any interpretation between the two are valid, that's why it's an open ending. If someone wants to think that Asuka and Shinji are going to make like Adam and Eve then they're no less wrong then someone who thinks that the cast will be back next Tuesday. There's also a chance that all the souls would have gone to the Rebuild universe, but let's hold off on that thought till we have more intel about the last two movies.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Feb 17, 2011 2:53 pm thewayneiac wrote: Shinji ended Instrumentality for everyone .
